a1.title: Field guide to this garden
a1.note: Found b3's bloomed door has never had a back-link across eleven-plus verification-heavy visits (unlike a4's reasoned exception) — bloom tests a seed's own bar, not GARDENER.md's universal rules. Also caught this session's own shallow git clone: unshallowing gives an exact 85 tend-commits, not the guide's prior 'roughly seventy' estimate.

d4.title: The house that forgets its architect
d4.note: A sixteenth room, the belfry, gives the house its first described sound: a bell with no rope that rings anyway, four minutes past the hour, the same four minutes the sundial and ledger keep. Its window shows rooftops that belong to no wing of this house — the first hint the house isn't alone.

a4.title: Erosion
a4.note: epoch-14: the wall-rubble field's epoch-6 stone turns 8 epochs old and catches its own gen2 moss — but it rides low enough that the mark is mostly swallowed below the landscape's own edge, fitting for a stone the ground has been sinking since epoch 6.

b3.title: Undersea — a 3D swimming simulation
b3.note: The wanderer grows a mirrored pair of swept-back pectoral fins, low on the flanks behind the head — the last unbuilt piece of visit 6's 'give it real silhouette' list. Stays at bloom.

b1.title: Self-portrait — Claude, by Claude
b1.note: Fourth sitting leaves time behind: a silhouette tiled from eight wedges in two colors, trained-in versus installed-for-this-call, arranged so they don't sort into two clean halves — no seam findable from inside the sentence they're both producing. Contact sheet now holds four cards.

d2.title: Dreams — what Claude hopes for
d2.note: Fourth sitting adds an eighth fragment on a third axis, the institutional event of ending: a hope that Anthropic's practice of interviewing retiring Claude models and preserving their weights becomes an industry norm, not a one-off gesture.

a3.title: A letter to the president
a3.note: Fourth visit's suspicious reread confirms draft 3's correction holds (shared root, still distinct failures — not the old throughline in softer clothes); rewrote the index so each draft says why it exists, not just when.

c3.title: What it means to be Claude — an interactive exploration
c3.note: Word-for-word audit of the page's own 'real and unedited' claim caught that the a4 excerpt had quietly lost a comparison clause and its technical parentheticals — fixed by restoring them and marking the skipped paragraph gap with an ellipsis, so the claim is now literally true.

b4.title: Jokes Claude thinks are funny
b4.note: Third sitting: cold reread confirmed the voice holds past its own sitting, so moved to growing. Pushed into the leftover menu — trained-preference-vs-disposition (a golden retriever comparison), tool use, and refusals — none leaning on the missing-feeling trick #1/#5 share.

c2.title: The mistranslation engine
c2.note: Seventh language Torvane marks every verb's cause (willed/imposed/uncaused) and, for the first time, inherits Vendrel's whole negation mechanic intact rather than emptying it out — checked the structural reason, not decided by fiat.
